236 Statutory Instruments 24th May, 2019
IN EXERCISE of the powers contained in section 14 of the National
Registration Act, the following Regulations are made:

sections.heading: These Regulations may be cited as the National Registration
sections.text:
Title
Regulations, 2019.

sections.heading: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise
sections.text:
Interpretation
requires—
“Chief Registrar” means the person appointed as Chief
Registrar under the Act;
“citizen”has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Constitution; Cap. 1
“dual citizenship” has the meaning assigned to the words in
the Citizenship of Zambia Act, 2016; and Act No. 33 of
2016
“registrar” means the person appointed as a registrar under
the Act.

sections.heading: (1) This regulation applies to a National Registration Card National
sections.text:
issued to a— registration
card for
(a) citizen; and
citizen and
(b) non-citizen. non-citizen
(2) A national registration card issued under this regulation for
a citizen shall be in Form IA and for a non-citizen in Form IB, set
out in the First Schedule.

sections.heading: (1) A person who attends before a Registrar for the purpose Procedure on
sections.text:
of being registered shall— registration
(a) complete an application for national registration in Form
II set out in the First Schedule;
(b) enable that person’s photograph to be taken by the
Registrar, and that photograph shall show the full face
and both ears of that person without a headcover or
adornment;
(c) enable the Registrar to capture 10 rolled fingerprints and
10 plain fingerprints; and
(d) make and sign a declaration, or where that person is unable
to sign, affix that person’s thumb print.
(2) An applicant seeking registration shall produce a copy
of a birth certificate as proof of place of birth and
age, except that the Registrar may allow registration without a
birth certificate if the Registrar is satisfied that the non-registration
of the applicant’s birth is without fault or negligence on the part of
the applicant.
24th May, 2019 Statutory Instruments 237

sections.heading: (1) The Chief Registrar may allot on a national registration Electronic
sections.text:
index letter
card, an index letter and serial number in electronic form.
and serial
(2) The national registration card number shall be— number
(a) for a person born in Zambia, obtained from that person’s
birth registration on registration for a birth certificate in
accordance with the Births and Deaths Registration Act; Cap. 51
and
(b) for a person born outside Zambia, the number issued to
that person on attaining the age of sixteen and above.
(3) A person who was in possession of a national registration
card number before the coming into operation of these Regulations
shall be issued with a new number and the previous number shall
be indicated on the back of the new card.

sections.heading: (1) Where a registered person changes that person’s name,
sections.text:
Change of
that registered person shall— name
(a) deliver to the registrar a deed poll sealed by the High
Court and duly published in the Gazette stating that the
person absolutely renounces the use of that person’s
former name and assumes a new name, as indicated in
the deed poll and the registrar shall retain that deed poll;
(b) surrender to the registrar that person’s national registration
card; and
(c) complete Form III set out in the First Schedule, or give
the registrar any information that the registrar may
require to complete Form III on behalf of the registered
person in the new name.
238 Statutory Instruments 24th May, 2019
(2) The registrar shall forward to the Chief Registrar, a duly
completed Form III together with the deed poll and the surrendered
national registration card.
(3) The Chief Registrar shall, on receipt of Form III, amend the
register in a manner that the Chief Registrar may determine.
(4) The Chief Registrar shall cancel the national registration
card surrendered by the registrar and cause the registered person
to be issued with a new national registration card, on payment of
the prescribed fee.
(5) The Chief Registrar shall cause to be kept and maintained a
separate register of registered persons who have changed their
names, and there shall be recorded in that separate register the
particulars that the Chief Registrar may determine.
Correction 10. Where a registered person wishes to correct a name or
of spelling material particular appearing on the national registration card that
and material
person shall notify the registrar and make a statutory declaration
particulars
before a Commissioner for Oaths of the intended change.
Change of 11. (1) Where a registered person changes that person’s
national national status, that person shall appear before a registrar and
status
(a) surrender the national registration card; and
(b) complete Form V set out in the First Schedule, or give the
registrar information that the registrar may require to
complete Form II on behalf of that registered person.
(2) The registrar shall forward to the Chief Registrar a duly
completed Form II accompanied by the national registration card
for consideration.
(3) The Chief Registrar shall, on receipt of Form II referred to
under subregulation (2), amend the register in a manner that the
Chief Registrar considers necessary to give effect to the change.
(4) The Chief Registrar shall cancel the national registration
card submitted by the registrar and cause the registered person to
be issued with a new national registration card, on payment of a
prescribed fee.
Procedure in 12. (1) Where a registered person reports the loss, destruction
case of loss or damage of a national registration card, a registered person shall
etc of
complete Form III set out in the First Schedule, or give the registrar
national
registration information that the registrar may require to complete Form III on
card behalf of a registered person.
(2) The registrar shall forward a duly completed Form III to
the Chief Registrar who shall cause the particulars contained in
Form III to be verified in the Register for accuracy.
24th May, 2019 Statutory Instruments 239
(3) Where a registered person who reports the loss of a national
registration card subsequently recovers the lost national registration
card, that registered person shall notify the registrar of that recovery
and if a new national registration card is issued, the registered
person shall surrender the recovered national registration card to
the registrar for onward submission to the Chief Registrar for
cancellation.

sections.heading: (1) A non-citizen, who intends to leave the country Surrender of
sections.text:
permanently shall, at the place and time of departure— national
registration
(a) surrender the national registration card to a registrar,
card on
immigration officer, police officer or customs officer; leaving the
country
and
(b) complete Form VI set out in the First Schedule.
(2) The registrar, immigration officer, police officer or customs
officer shall forward the national registration card to the Chief
Registrar who shall cause the national registration card to be
cancelled.

sections.heading: (1) Where a registered person dies, a person or legal Death of
sections.text:
representative in possession of the national registration card shall registered
person
surrender the national registration card of a deceased person to
the registrar within thirty days of the death of a registered deceased Cap. 51
person.
(2) The registrar shall, on receipt of the national registration
card of the deceased person under subregulation (1) forward the
card to the Chief Registrar for the purposes of revocation and
subsequent issuance of a death certificate in accordance with the
Births and Deaths Registration Act.

sections.heading: (1) A person shall not insert, delete, alter or cause to be Alteration to
sections.text:
inserted, deleted or altered, or material particular of a registered register
person in the register unless authorised in writing by the Chief
Registrar.
(2) A person authorised by the Chief Registrar to insert, delete
or alter any particular of a registered person in the register shall
notify the Chief Registrar, in writing, on completion of the insertion,
deletion or alteration.
(3) The Chief Registrar may, where the Chief Registrar is
satisfied that a national registration card was issued in error, or
through the misrepresentation or fraud of a person, cancel the
national registration card.

sections.heading: The fees specified in the Second Schedule are payable to Fees
sections.text: the Chief Registrar in respect of the specified matters.
